natural_occurrence.description: 4-Hexylresorcinol does not occur naturally. It is synthetically manufactured for use as an antioxidant and preservative, primarily in seafood.

manufacturing.description: Produced by alkylation of resorcinol with hexyl halide or similar organic synthesis routes. The resulting 4-hexylresorcinol is purified for food or pharmaceutical use.

other_uses.cosmetics: Skin lightening agent, anti-aging ingredient (inhibits melanin production).
other_uses.industrial: Limited industrial use outside of food and pharmaceuticals.
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Topical antiseptic, throat lozenges (historical use), wound care products.
other_uses.household: Some throat lozenges and antiseptic products.

eu.conditions: Restricted to use on fresh and frozen crustaceans to prevent melanosis (black spot). Specific maximum residue limits apply.

usa.notes: Approved for use on fresh shrimp to prevent melanosis, maximum 6 ppm.

japan.notes: Used on seafood with maximum residue limits.

canada.notes: Maximum 1 mg/kg on fresh or frozen shrimp.

regulatory_history.event: EFSA re-evaluated 4-hexylresorcinol and confirmed safety at current use levels for prevention of melanosis in crustaceans.

commonly_found_in: FRESH AND FROZEN SHRIMP (prevents black spots on shells and meat)
Some throat lozenges (pharmaceutical use, not food)
fun_facts: Prevents SHRIMP from turning BLACK after harvest - melanosis (black spot disease) makes shrimp look spoiled even when fresh
Works by inhibiting polyphenol oxidase enzyme - the same enzyme that makes apples turn brown when cut
Without it, shrimp would develop unsightly black spots within hours, even if perfectly fresh and safe to eat
Also used in THROAT LOZENGES as a mild antiseptic (though this use has declined)
Found in some SKIN LIGHTENING cosmetics because it inhibits melanin production
The melanosis it prevents is purely cosmetic - doesn't affect safety or nutritional value of shrimp
Discovered in the 1920s and used medicinally before its food application was developed
Very small amounts needed - effective at just 1-6 parts per million
